content: react-i18next pitfalls: (1) Translation missing in production: if not ready state is not handled before rendering, saveMissing fires even though keys exist — just not yet loaded, producing false missing-key logs and blank UI. Solution: wrap app in Suspense or check i18n.isInitialized. (2) Re-render storms: putting i18n instance into React context causes full tree re-render on language change; prefer useTranslation hook with specific namespace. (3) TypeScript key safety: i18next supports full recursive key type inference via resources augmentation in d.ts file — catches typos at compile time. Must set strict:true in tsconfig. (4) Pluralization: Polish has complex plural rules (one/few/many/other) — i18next handles them if the correct language code is detected, but release builds sometimes drop debug resolvers causing parseMissingKeyHandler to fire for valid plural keys; test plural keys explicitly. (5) Namespace lazy loading: large translation files loaded upfront slow the first paint; split into at least 'common' and 'report' namespaces.
